diagrams.title: Binary Tree Structure and Terminology
diagrams.description: A labeled binary tree diagram showing all key terminology: root node, parent, left child, right child, leaf nodes, and subtrees. This visual reference helps students identify and name each component of a binary tree.
diagrams.purpose: Define the fundamental terminology and structure of a binary tree including node relationships and tree components

diagrams.title: Tree Height and Levels
diagrams.description: A binary tree diagram showing levels (root at level 0), height computation, and the relationship between levels. Nodes are arranged by level with clear level numbering to illustrate depth and height concepts.
diagrams.purpose: Explain the concepts of tree height, depth, and levels, and demonstrate how nodes are distributed across levels

diagrams.title: Types of Binary Trees
diagrams.description: Three side-by-side binary tree illustrations showing: (1) Full/Proper binary tree - every node has 0 or 2 children, (2) Complete binary tree - all levels filled except possibly last, (3) Degenerate/Skewed tree - each node has only one child forming a linked list.
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the different types of binary trees and their structural properties to help students distinguish between them
